name: Stuffed & Grilled Corn Husks
recipeIngredient: 2 large red potatoes, boiled, peeled and diced
1 cup canned black beans, rinsed and drained
salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 cup grated monterey jack cheese, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Carefully remove the husks of each ear of corn, taking care not to rip the widest husks. Select 24 widest husks and set aside, covered with damp paper towels. Cut 2 remaining husks into 8 long strips and reserve, covered with damp paper towels.
2. Cut corn kernels from each cob and reserve.
3. In a large bowl whisk together lime juice, garlic, jalapeno, cumin and cayenne. Slowly add 6 tablespoons olive oil in a stream, whisking constantly.
4. Add corn, potatoes, black beans, red bell pepper and season with salt and pepper, tossing to combine and coat with the dressing. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
5. Add cilantro leaves, stirring to combine.
6. Place 2 corn husks, concave side up, on a work surface, overlapping them at the wider ends by 2 inches. Center a third husk on top of the first two husks.
7. Arrange about half cup vegetable mixture in the center of the husks.
8. Fold sides of the corn husks up and over the filling to cover it completely. Tie either end with a corn husk strip to secure the filling, forming cigar-shaped packets.
9. Make more packets using remaining ingredients. Packets can be made up to 4 hours in advance.
10. Preheat grill or grill pan.
11. Brush packets with remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil and grill each packet for 6 minutes per side, covered. If desired, cut each packet open and sprinkle grated cheese over vegetables. Return packets to grill or broil until cheese is melted. Serve immediately.
12. Yield: 4 servings
